description: If you hurry too much, you find yourself behind soon.

(;B[dq];W[eq]C[Black can't get a goot result by playing like this.]))

(;B[fs];W[fr]C[Black cannot make more points than in the solution by playing like this.])

(;B[fs];W[fr]C[Ko. Even if white loses it and connects at H1, this will be still one point worse than the solution.])
(;B[fr];W[fs];B[es]C[];W[hs]C[Two points worse than the solution.]))
(;B[fo];W[fp];B[ep];W[gs];B[fs];W[fr]C[Very hard ko for black. And even if white loses it and connects at H1, the result will still be one point worse than the solution.])
(;B[fs];W[fo];B[eo];W[fp];B[ep];W[hs];B[gs]C[You didn't need to play the square marked stone. In some rules ( for example ING rules) it would do no harm in this situation (when sente is not important), but in Japanese rules it is minus one point.]MA[fs]))
source: Thinking about something totally go unrelated
